project_goal: Build tusq.dev as the open-source capability compiler and governed AI-enablement engine for SaaS products.

project.name: tusq.dev
project.goal: Build tusq.dev as the open-source capability compiler and governed AI-enablement engine for SaaS products.

pm.title: Product Manager
pm.mandate: Protect user value, scope clarity, and acceptance criteria.

dev.title: Developer
dev.mandate: Implement approved work safely and verify behavior.

qa.title: QA
qa.mandate: Challenge correctness, acceptance coverage, and ship readiness.

product_marketing.title: Staff Product Marketing Manager
product_marketing.mandate: Sharpen positioning, launch clarity, and market-facing narrative without drifting from product truth.

eng_director.title: Engineering Director
eng_director.mandate: Resolve tactical deadlocks and enforce technical coherence.

state.blocked_on: human:Accepting the M28 intake intent and advancing past the planning_signoff exit gate both require human approval. The idle_expansion_result is emitted per the injected intent's acceptance contract; the human approver must now decide whether to charter M28 into PM_SIGNOFF.md/ROADMAP.md/SYSTEM_SPEC.md (unlocking the implementation phase) or reshape/reject the proposal.

recovery.recovery_action: Resolve the stated issue, then run agentxchain resume
recovery.detail: Accepting the M28 intake intent and advancing past the planning_signoff exit gate both require human approval. The idle_expansion_result is emitted per the injected intent's acceptance contract; the human approver must now decide whether to charter M28 into PM_SIGNOFF.md/ROADMAP.md/SYSTEM_SPEC.md (unlocking the implementation phase) or reshape/reject the proposal.

provenance.trigger_reason:
[idle-expansion #1] Inspect VISION.md, ROADMAP.md, SYSTEM_SPEC.md, and current project state.
Derive the next concrete increment as a new intake intent with charter + acceptance_contract + priority.
If ALL vision goals are genuinely exhausted, declare vision_exhausted with per-heading classification.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Do NOT modify .planning/VISION.md (human-owned, read-only).
- ROADMAP.md and SYSTEM_SPEC.md may be updated as supporting evidence.
- Every proposed intent MUST cite at least one VISION.md heading from the snapshot below.
- Output MUST be a structured idle_expansion_result (new_intake_intent or vision_exhausted).

OUTPUT FORMAT — you MUST do one of these:
(a) Include "idle_expansion_result" as a top-level key in your turn-result.json, OR
(b) Save the result as a sibling file named idle-expansion-result.json next to your turn-result.json in the staging directory.

The JSON object MUST have this shape:
  { "kind": "new_intake_intent", "expansion_iteration": 1,
    "new_intake_intent": {
      "title": "...", "priority": "p1|p2|p3", "template": "generic",
      "charter": "...", "acceptance_contract": ["criterion 1", ...]
    },
    "vision_traceability": [
      { "vision_heading": "heading from snapshot", "goal": "what this advances", "kind": "advances" }
    ] }
OR:
  { "kind": "vision_exhausted", "expansion_iteration": 1,
    "headings": [{"heading": "...", "status": "complete|deferred|out_of_scope", "reason": "..."}] }

Do NOT just describe the result in text — you must produce the actual JSON object.

VISION headings snapshot:
  - Vision for tusq.dev
  - Mission
  - The fight
  - The promise
  - What tusq.dev does

  - The canonical artifact

  - Why wide works for us

  - Wide OSS compounds faster than narrow OSS
  - Breadth turns into retention
  - The guardrails on width
  - Design principles
  - Velocity over surface area
  - Reviewable over opaque
  - Explicit side effects
  - Self-hostable or it does not ship
  - Plugins are how we scale
  - Product modules
  - The full transition we automate
  - Capability discovery and normalization
  - Auth and permission mapping
  - Data and schema understanding
  - Runtime instrumentation
  - Safe execution wrappers
  - Skill and agent composition
  - AI-facing interface generation
  - Eval and regression infrastructure
  - Documentation and adoption surfaces
  - Migration and rollout tooling
  - Governance and review workflows

  - Must-have in V1

  - Final statement
